title: Deepen the linter run-checks module
description:
Selected candidate from architecture review of pi-quality-gates (report at /var/folders/b8/d83y6l9x4y36fx19rwkbn4340000gn/T/architecture-review-20260614-001.html).

**Current shallow interface**
src/linter/core.ts mixes config loading, markdownlint specifics, CLI runner, project-root discovery, output parsing, code-excerpt generation, and outcome merging. Callers (src/linter/index.ts and tests) must reach into many exported helpers to use it, and the module has no single seam for "run checks on these files".

**Target depth**
A LinterPipeline module that exposes a small interface:
- runChecks(filePaths: string[]) -> ValidationOutcome
- summarize(outcome: ValidationOutcome, options?) -> bounded message
- persist(report: string) -> sidecar metadata

Behind that seam, hide:
- config loading (LinterConfigLoader)
- linter adapters (markdownlint adapter, CLI adapter, LSP adapter)
- report parser / excerpt builder
- outcome merger

**Affected files/modules**
- src/linter/core.ts (split)
- src/linter/index.ts (call through new seam)
- src/linter/lsp.ts (becomes an adapter behind the seam)
- src/linter/report-hygiene.ts (summary builder / sidecar consumer)
- test/index.test.ts (replace __test__ internals with pipeline seam tests)

**Out of scope (for this epic)**
- Shared sidecar store consolidation with reviewer (Candidate 2; can be a follow-up)
- Reviewer context seams (Candidate 3)
- LSP client/server refactoring
- New linter types (only restructuring existing ones)

**Validation and closeout expectations**
- Characterization tests added for existing behaviour before restructuring
- All existing tests pass
- New tests exercise the pipeline seam with fake adapters
- No behaviour change for default config and CLI usage
- Feature branch + PR
closeReason: LinterPipeline refactor complete. All child issues closed, PR #2 opened and is clean/mergeable.

title: Unify the report sidecar module
description:
Selected candidate from architecture review (report at /tmp/architecture-review-20260615-091939.html).

**Selected candidate**: Unify the duplicated report sidecar logic shared by the post-turn-linter and post-turn-reviewer.

**Affected files/modules**:
- src/linter/report-hygiene.ts (currently ~631 lines)
- src/reviewer/report-hygiene.ts (currently ~343 lines)
- src/linter/index.ts (consumes writeLinterReportSidecar, recoverLinterReportSidecar)
- src/reviewer/index.ts (consumes writeReviewerReportSidecar, recoverReviewerReportSidecar)
- tests that exercise sidecar write/recover/redaction

**Current shallow interface / leaky seam**:
Both subsystems duplicate the same storage contract: redaction, path sanitization, SHA-256/byte accounting, temp-file write, preview/slice/full recovery, and default sidecar directory resolution. The duplication is a shallow seam: the interface is "each subsystem knows how to store its own reports," which leaks storage policy into two places.

**Target depth, locality, and leverage**:
A single shared report-sidecar module owns storage policy and redaction. Linter and reviewer become adapters that supply only tool-specific metadata (toolName, summaryMode) and summary formatting. Callers get leverage because new sidecar features (e.g., compression, encryption-at-rest, retention) apply to both consumers automatically. Maintainers get locality because redaction rules and size limits live in one file.

**Likely seam placement**:
New module at src/shared/report-sidecar.ts exposes a generic storage interface. Linter-specific and reviewer-specific typed wrappers live in src/linter/report-hygiene.ts and src/reviewer/report-hygiene.ts, which are reduced to summary formatting and argument parsing.

**Explicitly out of scope**:
- Changing the redaction rules (reuse existing logic, move only).
- Changing the sidecar file format or directory layout.
- Refactoring summary formatting beyond what is required to keep callers clean.
- Splitting reviewer.ts or deleting linter/core.ts (separate candidates).

**Validation and PR closeout expectations**:
- Existing sidecar tests in test/index.test.ts must pass.
- New characterization tests for the shared module should be added before/while moving code.
- npm run typecheck and npm run test must pass.
- Work on a feature branch; open a PR when ready.
closeReason: All plan steps completed, PR #3 opened, validation passed, decision recorded in Mulch.

title: Deepen the reviewer extension entry point
description:
Selected candidate from architecture review of pi-quality-gates (report at /var/folders/b8/d83y6l9x4y36fx19rwkbn4340000gn/T/architecture-review-20260615-112020.html).

**Current shallow interface**
src/reviewer/index.ts mixes lifecycle hooks (session_start, session_shutdown, turn_end), a state machine (IDLE → GATHERING → REVIEWING → FIX_REQUESTED → RE_REVIEWING → IDLE), Pi command registration, report formatting, sidecar persistence, and fix-request scheduling. Callers (the extension wiring and tests) must understand the internal state shape and transition rules.

**Target depth**
A ReviewerOrchestrator module that exposes a small interface such as:
- onLinterClean(files: string[], timestamp?: number): void
- onTurnEnd(ctx: ExtensionContext): Promise<void>
- requestReview(ctx: ExtensionContext, options?: { isReReview?: boolean }): Promise<void>
- registerCommands(pi: ExtensionAPI): void
- getStateSnapshot(): ReviewerStateSnapshot

Behind that seam, hide:
- state machine transitions
- report formatting / summary builders
- reviewer sidecar writes
- fix-request scheduling and loop counting
- configuration and skip-filter loading

**Affected files/modules**
- src/reviewer/index.ts (becomes thin extension wiring)
- new src/reviewer/orchestrator.ts (state machine + lifecycle surface)
- src/reviewer/reviewer.ts (likely ReviewPipeline follow-up; keep as adapter behind seam for now)
- src/reviewer/report-hygiene.ts (summary formatting remains tool-specific)
- test files exercising reviewer behavior

**Out of scope (for this epic)**
- Deep reviewer core pipeline refactor (Candidate 3; can follow)
- Linter controller refactor (Candidate 2)
- Shared change-detection module (Candidate 4)
- New reviewer domains / checklists

**Validation and closeout expectations**
- Characterization tests added for existing turn_end and command behavior before restructuring
- All existing tests pass
- New tests exercise the orchestrator seam with a fake ReviewPipeline
- No behavior change for default config and CLI usage
- Feature branch + PR

title: Deepen the reviewer execution module
description:
Selected candidate from architecture review of pi-quality-gates (report at /var/folders/b8/d83y6l9x4y36fx19rwkbn4340000gn/T/architecture-review-20260615-124527.html).

**Selected candidate**: Deepen the reviewer execution module.

**Affected files/modules**:
- src/reviewer/reviewer.ts (currently exposes diff/filter/prompt/spawn/parse helpers as the caller-facing surface)
- src/reviewer/orchestrator.ts (current caller of execution mechanics)
- src/reviewer/index.ts (thin wiring that currently assembles default execution dependencies)
- tests that exercise reviewer execution and orchestrator behavior

**Current shallow interface / leaky seam**:
src/reviewer/reviewer.ts has a toolbox interface nearly as complex as its implementation: gatherDiff, applyDiffFilters, renderTaskTemplate, spawnReviewer, parseReviewReport, hasFindingsAboveThreshold, and display formatting. Callers must know the sequence and error modes for diff gathering, prompt rendering, child Pi execution, output parsing, and review result interpretation.

**Target depth, locality, and leverage**:
A reviewer execution module should own the end-to-end behavior behind one seam: collect review context, apply file filters, render prompts, run the reviewer child process, parse the output, and return a normalized reviewer result. Callers get leverage by asking for review execution rather than assembling mechanics. Maintainers get locality because prompt/diff/spawn/parse failures are isolated.

**Likely seam placement**:
A new or adjusted reviewer execution seam near src/reviewer/reviewer.ts, consumed by src/reviewer/orchestrator.ts and configured by src/reviewer/index.ts. Detailed interface choice is intentionally unresolved until design interrogation.

**Explicitly out of scope**:
- Changing reviewer prompt content or review domains.
- Changing report sidecar storage format.
- Changing ReviewerOrchestrator state-machine behavior except where needed to call the new seam.
- Linter controller refactor.
- LSP diagnostics refactor.

**Validation and PR closeout expectations**:
- Characterization tests cover existing reviewer execution behavior before restructuring.
- Tests cross the same reviewer execution seam as callers.
- npm run typecheck and npm run test pass.
- Work stays on a feature branch and opens a PR before closeout.
closeReason: PR #5 opened after validation and read-only review pass; reviewer execution seam refactor complete.

title: Deepen the linter extension controller
description:
Selected candidate from architecture review of pi-quality-gates (report at /var/folders/b8/d83y6l9x4y36fx19rwkbn4340000gn/T/architecture-review-20260615-124527.html).

**Selected candidate**: Deepen the linter extension controller.

**Affected files/modules**:
- src/linter/index.ts (currently combines Pi lifecycle wiring, modified-file detection, cooldown/dedupe state, sidecar recovery, status messages, and fix-turn scheduling)
- src/linter/pipeline.ts (already deep run-checks seam; should remain the execution dependency)
- src/linter/report-hygiene.ts (summary/sidecar helpers used by controller)
- tests that exercise linter commands, fix prompts, and post-turn behavior

**Current shallow interface / leaky seam**:
src/linter/index.ts is an 853-line extension entry point. It mixes Pi event registration with state-machine/controller behavior. Tests reach into __test__ helper exports for file detection, tokenization, and createPostTurnLinter.

**Target depth, locality, and leverage**:
A linter controller/orchestrator module should own lifecycle state, queued file handling, cooldown, dedupe, latest report, sidecar recovery, and fix-turn policy behind a small event/command seam. The extension entry point should become thin Pi wiring, symmetric with the reviewer orchestrator pattern.

**Likely seam placement**:
A new src/linter/orchestrator.ts or controller module consumed by src/linter/index.ts. Detailed interface choice will be confirmed before plan submission.

**Explicitly out of scope**:
- Changing linter adapter behavior or LinterPipeline semantics.
- Changing report sidecar storage format.
- LSP diagnostics refactor.
- Reviewer refactors.

**Validation and PR closeout expectations**:
- Characterization tests cover existing linter lifecycle/command/fix behavior before restructuring.
- Tests cross the same controller seam as callers where practical.
- npm run typecheck and npm run test pass.
- Work stays on a feature branch and opens a PR before closeout.
closeReason: Plan pl-b26b succeeded; PR opened at https://github.com/davehardy20/pi-quality-gates/pull/6 with validation and review complete.

title: Implement container-backed PR reviewer runtime
description:
Execution profile: manual implementation in current session; no orchestrator/subagent plan for this phase.

Goal: wire /pr-review to execute reviewer attempts in a populated Apple container runtime (node-ts-fuller for TypeScript) with hydrated workspace, validation tooling, visible status, and transcript diagnostics. Replace the current local child Pi reviewer path or add a fail-closed fallback.

Closeout gate: PR-first workflow; push/open PR only after local validation and gate handling.

title: Track blockers for container-backed LLM reviewer runtime
description:
## STATUS: DEFERRED (2026-06-16)

Parked by explicit decision. The local headless-pi reviewer path works as intended and remains the chosen runtime for now. This issue is parked, not closed, so the research below survives.

**Preconditions to reopen (all required):**
1. Orchestrator system stabilises (currently WIP in pi-config-tests).
2. The Apple Container sandbox launcher (`prepareSandboxSpawn` + `loadSandboxConfig` + provider-proxy + worker-lease + runtime-manifest + launch-plan) is split out of the orchestrator extension into a shared importable package so pi-quality-gates can consume it without duplicating the proxy.
3. The pi-apple-sandbox extraction is something Dave explicitly wants at that point.

**Key finding from Track A research (don't re-derive):** Reusing the existing wheel is NOT a small change. The launcher lives as private TS source inside pi-config-tests/agent/extensions/shared/ — it is not shipped anywhere importable (not in the published pi SDK, not in ~/.pi/agent/npm, not its own package). pi-quality-gates has zero references to it. So "reuse the wheel" first requires creating the packaging boundary. Once that exists, the pi-quality-gates change is ~20 lines: `if loadSandboxConfig().enabled → prepareSandboxSpawn(piArgs) → spawn(launchPlan)`; else local fallback.

Note: pi-config-tests branch `nyx/post-turn-linter-continue-after-fix` has a small uncommitted proxy robustness fix (writeJson/destroyed-response guards + isIgnorableConnectionError + socket error sinks for ECONNRESET/EPIPE when sandboxed workers disconnect). Track A would benefit from that landing; it is NOT a rewrite (the large diff-stat is whitespace churn).

When reopened, resume from "export the launcher as a shared package," NOT from "build a proxy." The proxy already exists and works.

---

Parent: pi-quality-gates-cc4b (Implement container-backed PR reviewer runtime).

## Original context (preserved)

Phase 1 (commits 11c4dba4, d0164320, 6ba87279; merged via PR #15) shipped Apple-container validation evidence collection and made /pr-review fail-closed: it refuses to stamp PASS from the local headless reviewer (allowLocalReviewerFallback defaults false) and returns CANNOT_REVIEW when container validation fails.

TRACK A — Runtime / auth: container Pi has no provider auth; needs runtime-bundle + host /llm proxy (no raw ~/.pi mount).
TRACK B — Workspace hydration: writable workspace copy + npm ci/install hydration step; remaining = npm internal error in image, macOS-native host deps, biome not baked (currently skipped not failed).

Done when: container Pi completes a headless LLM call via /llm proxy without raw secrets, AND runContainerValidationEvidence returns status=passed for this repo; then replace local reviewerExecution with container-backed backend.

title: Retire the post-turn-reviewer auto-trigger module
description:
Retire the auto-triggering post-turn-reviewer state machine that never worked as intended, now that pr-gate (/pr-review + push gate) is the chosen review path.

RETIRE (~1,951 LOC across src/reviewer/):
- index.ts (extension entry, turn_end auto-trigger)
- orchestrator.ts (927 — ReviewerOrchestrator: session state, fix/re-review loop, sidecar writes, /reviewer-* commands)
- config.ts (.pi/reviewer.config.json loader)
- commands/reviewer-model.ts (/reviewer-model command)
- report-hygiene.ts (post-turn-reviewer sidecar wrappers)
- prompts/system.md, prompts/task-template.md

KEEP (pr-gate imports these across the folder boundary — verified zero dependency on the retiring layer):
- reviewer.ts (spawnReviewer, createReviewerExecution, formatReportForDisplay)
- types.ts (ReviewConfig contract, also used by pr-gate/pr-review-config.ts)
- reviewer-skip.ts (loadSkipFilter)

ALSO TOUCH:
- src/index.ts (remove postTurnReviewerExtension import + call)
- test/reviewer-orchestrator.test.ts (retire whole file with orchestrator)
- test/index.test.ts (surgery: remove orchestrator __test__ import + report-hygiene test blocks/imports; keep reviewer.ts/reviewer-skip test blocks if still relevant to pr-gate)
- README.md (drop Post-Turn Reviewer section, workflow diagram, reviewer config block, /reviewer-status|run|model|toggle commands)

OUT OF SCOPE (deferred to a separate candidate): relocating the keep-layer (reviewer.ts/types.ts/reviewer-skip.ts) into pr-gate/; promoting ReviewConfig into shared/.

CONSTRAINTS:
- pr-gate must keep building and its tests green (it reuses reviewer.ts/types.ts/reviewer-skip.ts unchanged).
- No shared/ module should become an orphan (linter still consumes report-sidecar, review-scope, review-severity, review-types).
- Commands /reviewer-status, /reviewer-run, /reviewer-model, /reviewer-toggle are removed from the extension surface.

VALIDATION: run_typecheck (src+test), run_vitest (full), build. PR-first closeout on a feature branch.

MULCH AFTER SUCCESS: record retirement decision; note mx-eb899a evidence deleted (pattern survives via linter/pr-gate) and mx-46fdf3 orchestrator-half voided (ReviewerExecution-half stays valid); mx-87a9dd unaffected (spawnReviewer kept, pr-gate still uses it).

title: Deepen the markdownlint adapter: move the engine out of config-loader
description:
## Candidate C2 (chosen from architecture review 2026-06-17)

The markdownlint `LinterAdapter` (37 LOC) is a shallow pass-through; the real
engine (`runMarkdownlint`, ignore-file discovery/loading/filtering, fix + result
formatting — ~330 LOC) lives inside the mis-named `config-loader.ts` (521 LOC).
This violates convention `mx-295f57` (adapters should receive config, not run
linting on the wrong side of the seam) and leaves markdownlint behaviour with no
test crossing the `LinterAdapter` seam.

## Affected modules
- `src/linter/adapters/markdownlint.ts` (37) — becomes thin-over-deep
- `src/linter/config-loader.ts` (521) — sheds the engine, keeps pure config
- `src/linter/core.ts` — deprecated re-export shim, follows the moved symbols
- `src/linter/types.ts` / `src/linter/adapters/types.ts` — interfaces, unchanged
- Tests: `test/index.test.ts`, `test/linter-pipeline.test.ts`

## Current shallow interface / leaky seam
- `createMarkdownlintAdapter({runner})` defers entirely to a pre-configured
  `runMarkdownlint` closure it is handed. It never sees `MarkdownlintConfig`.
- `config-loader.ts::attachMarkdownlintConfig` bakes user config into the runner
  closure at load time; `DEFAULT_CONFIG.linters[".md"].runner = runMarkdownlint`.
- markdownlint depth (execution + ignore + formatting) is colocated with config
  loading — two responsibilities in one module.

## Target outcome
- New deep module `src/linter/markdownlint.ts` owns: `runMarkdownlint`,
  `filterExistingFiles`, `findMarkdownlintIgnore`,
  `loadMarkdownlintIgnorePatterns`, `filterIgnoredFiles`,
  `formatMarkdownlintFixInfo`, `formatMarkdownlintResults`,
  `DEFAULT_MARKDOWNLINT_CONFIG`.
- `config-loader.ts` keeps pure config: `DEFAULT_CONFIG`, `parseJsoncConfig`,
  `loadMarkdownlintConfig`, `loadLinterConfig`, `attachMarkdownlintConfig`,
  `getLinterForFile`, `MAX_MODIFIED_FILES`, type re-exports. It imports
  `runMarkdownlint` from the new module (config wires the runner; engine stays
  behind the seam).
- Adapter becomes thin-over-deep and exercisable through the `LinterAdapter`
  seam (the real test surface).

## Likely seam placement
`src/linter/markdownlint.ts` (single file, not a folder). `LinterAdapter`
interface stays in `adapters/types.ts`. The adapter factory stays in
`adapters/markdownlint.ts`.

## Benefits
- **Test surface** — markdownlint becomes exercisable through the adapter seam
  (today only through the deprecated `core.ts` entry).
- **Locality** — engine sits with its adapter.
- **Convention** — restores `mx-295f57` (adapters receive config / own their
  execution, not handed a pre-baked runner closure).

## Out of scope
- Removing the `core.ts` deprecated shim (separate migration; it keeps re-exporting).
- CLI / LSP adapters (their own candidates).
- C1 (redraw `shared/`), C3 (reviewer relocation), C4 (pr-gate entry split).
- `remark-lint-poc` (C5 — may fold in later, not now).

## Validation
`run_typecheck` (src + test), `run_vitest` (full suite), build. PR-first
closeout on a feature branch.

## Open design decision (resolved in plan)
Adapter config wiring: (A) keep runner-injection (minimal) vs (B) config-
injection (idiomatic, makes `mx-295f57` fully true). See plan.

## Report reference
`/var/folders/.../architecture-review-20260617-112422.html` (temporary).
